title: Fruit scones
subtitle: Classic scones with sultanas
excerpt: Fruit scones follow the same method as plain scones with sultanas folded in at the end. The fruit is added after the milk so it doesn't bleed colour into the dough. Soak the sultanas in hot water for 10 minutes first if they are particularly dry.
sourceNotes: Mrs Beeton, Book of Household Management (1861), Project Gutenberg #10136 — currant and fruit scones appear in the bread and cakes chapters as a standard variant on plain scone dough. The sultana scone is the most common British form.

recipe.freezeNotes: Freeze baked and cooled. Warm from frozen at 180°C for 8 minutes.
recipe.batchNotes: Best eaten the day they are baked.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Cut and freeze unbaked. Bake from frozen at 220°C, adding 3–4 minutes.

glossaryTerms.definition: The step of soaking dried fruit in hot water, tea, or juice for 10–15 minutes before adding it to a batter or dough. Dry, shrivelled fruit absorbs moisture from the surrounding mixture during baking and can make the finished product dry and tough. Plumped fruit goes in fully hydrated, which keeps the crumb around it moist.

content.text: The method is identical to plain scones with the addition of sultanas. Dry, old sultanas will pull moisture from the scone crumb and make it tough —
in hot water for 10 minutes before using them prevents this. Drain and pat dry before adding.

attrs.prepNote: plumped in hot water 10 minutes, drained

content.text: Heat the oven to 220°C (200°C fan). Sift {{self-raising-flour}}, {{baking-powder}}, and {{salt-table}} into a large bowl. Rub in {{unsalted-butter}} until the mixture resembles breadcrumbs. Stir in {{caster-sugar}}. Pour in nearly all the {{whole-milk}} and mix quickly to a soft dough. Fold through the drained {{sultanas}}. Pat out to 3 cm thick on a floured surface and stamp out rounds with a 6 cm cutter, pressing straight down.
Place on a lined tray and brush the tops with beaten {{eggs}}. Bake for 10–12 minutes until risen and deep gold on top. Eat within a few hours of baking.
